title: Youssef Chouhoud
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Youssef Chouhoud
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
biography: Dr. Youssef Chouhoud is an Associate Professor of Political Science affiliated with the Reiff Center for Human Rights and Conflict Resolution at Christopher Newport University. Additionally, he is a Public Fellow with the Public Religion Research Institute, producing accessible scholarship on religious, racial, and ethnic pluralism in the US. Dr. Chouhoud's research models support for core democratic norms, with a particular focus on tolerance and a more general interest in how religiosity mediates social and political attitudes. He also has an extensive record of public scholarship on Muslim and Arab American opinions and behaviors. Prior to joining CNU, Dr. Chouhoud was a Provost’s Fellow at the University of Southern California, where he received his Ph.D. As a President’s Scholar, he earned his M.A. in Political Science at Lehigh University, where he also received a B.A. in History.
education_entries: Ph D in Political Science and Int'l Relations, University of Southern California
MA in Political Science, Lehigh University
research_interests: Comparative political attitudes and behavior; religion, race, and ethnic politics; Muslim minorities in the West; survey methodology
teaching_interests: Middle East politics; comparative politics; democracy and democratization
